briefdescription.para: The following constants map to the XPAR parameters created in the xparameters.h file.

detaileddescription.para: They are defined here such that a user can easily change all the needed parameters in one place.

briefdescription.para: The following constant defines the address of the IIC device on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: Note that since the address is only 7 bits, this constant is the address divided by 2.

briefdescription.para: Maximum delay count used for timeout handling.
Internal event value used for IIC event checking.
The instance of the IIC device.
Write buffer for writing a page.
Read buffer for reading a page.
Flag to check completion of Transmission.
This function writes and reads the data to the IIC Slave.
This function writes a buffer of data to IIC Slave.

parameterdescription.para: contains the number of bytes in the buffer to be written.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a data from the IIC Slave into a specified buffer.

parameterdescription.para: contains the address of the data buffer to be filled.
contains the number of bytes to be read.

briefdescription.para: This Send handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that data in the specified buffer has been sent.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC device instance.
is the number of bytes sent.

briefdescription.para: This Receive handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that data in the specified buffer has been received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC device instance.
is the number of bytes received.

briefdescription.para: This Status handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates the events that have occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC driver instance for which the handler is being called for.
indicates the condition that has occurred.

briefdescription.para: Flag to check completion of Reception.

detaileddescription.para: Main function to call the Repeated Start example.

briefdescription.para: This file consists of a interrupt mode design example to demonstrate the use of repeated start using the XIic driver.

detaileddescription.para: The XIic_MasterSend() API is used to transmit the data and XIic_MasterRecv() API is used to receive the data.
The IIC devices that are present on the Xilinx boards do not support the repeated start option. These examples have been tested with an IIC device external to the boards.
This code assumes that no Operating System is being used.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a mta 02/20/06 Created. 2.00a sdm 09/22/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs, replaced call to XIic_Initialize API with XIic_LookupConfig and XIic_CfgInitialize. Updated the example with a fix for CR539763 where XIic_Start was being called instead of XIic_Stop. Added code for setting up the StatusHandler callback. 3.4 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 3.10 gm 07/09/23 Added SDT support 3.15 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues 3.15 vlt 02/17/26 Added timeout handling to polled wait loops.
